name: 6. 고등 화법 전환 150
description: 평서문/의문문(Yes-No·wh-)/명령문 간접화법, 시제 일치 규칙, 인칭·부사 변화(today→that day 등), 특수 화법(감탄·기원·제안) 150개 (고등 수준)

words.w: He said, "I am tired." → He said that he was tired.
words.m: [평서문 간접화법] 현재 → 과거 (직접 → 간접, 시제 일치)

words.w: She said, "I like apples." → She said that she liked apples.
words.m: [평서문 간접화법] 그녀는 자기가 사과를 좋아한다고 말했다.

words.w: He said to me, "I'll help you." → He told me that he would help me.
words.m: [평서문 간접화법] 그는 나를 돕겠다고 내게 말했다. (said to → told)

words.w: She said, "I'm studying." → She said that she was studying.
He said, "I went to Paris." → He said that he had gone to Paris.
She said, "I have seen that movie." → She said she had seen that movie.
He said, "I can swim." → He said he could swim.
She said, "I will come tomorrow." → She said she would come the next day.
words.m: [평서문 간접화법] will → would, tomorrow → the next day

words.w: He told me, "I'm busy." → He told me that he was busy.
She said, "I don't know." → She said that she didn't know.
He said, "I met her yesterday." → He said he had met her the day before.
She said, "I love you." → She said that she loved me.
He said, "I want to leave." → He said he wanted to leave.
She said, "It's raining." → She said it was raining.
He said, "I'm a teacher." → He said that he was a teacher.
She said, "We are friends." → She said that they were friends.
He said, "I'll call you." → He said he would call me.
She said, "I ate lunch." → She said she had eaten lunch.
He said, "I have a dog." → He said he had a dog.
She said, "I feel sick." → She said she felt sick.
words.m: [평서문 간접화법] 그녀는 몸이 안 좋다고 말했다.

words.w: He said, "I must go." → He said he had to go.
She said, "I've been there." → She said she had been there.
words.m: [평서문 간접화법] 그녀는 거기 가 본 적 있다고 말했다.

words.w: He told her, "I'll wait for you." → He told her that he would wait for her.
She said, "I'm leaving now." → She said she was leaving then.
He said, "I saw you yesterday." → He said he had seen me the day before.
words.m: [평서문 간접화법] 그는 전날 나를 봤다고 말했다.

words.w: She said, "I always drink coffee." → She said she always drank coffee.
words.m: [평서문 간접화법] 그녀는 항상 커피를 마신다고 말했다.

words.w: He said, "I'm happy here." → He said that he was happy there.
She said, "I've finished." → She said she had finished.
He said, "I know the answer." → He said he knew the answer.
She said, "I'm tired today." → She said she was tired that day.
He said, "I live in Seoul." → He said he lived in Seoul.
She said, "I'll arrive soon." → She said she would arrive soon.
He said, "I can't help." → He said he couldn't help.
words.m: [평서문 간접화법] 그는 도울 수 없다고 말했다.

words.w: She said, "I love this book." → She said she loved that book.
He said, "I don't understand." → He said he didn't understand.
She said, "I've been waiting." → She said she had been waiting.
He said, "I know her well." → He said he knew her well.
words.m: [평서문 간접화법] 그는 그녀를 잘 안다고 말했다.

words.w: She said, "I'm ready." → She said she was ready.
He said, "I didn't do it." → He said he hadn't done it.
She said, "I feel good." → She said she felt good.
He asked, "Do you like coffee?" → He asked if I liked coffee.
She asked, "Are you ready?" → She asked if I was ready.
He asked, "Can you swim?" → He asked if I could swim.
words.m: [의문문 간접화법] 그는 내가 수영할 수 있는지 물었다.

words.w: She asked, "Have you seen it?" → She asked if I had seen it.
He asked, "Will you come?" → He asked if I would come.
She asked, "Did you call?" → She asked if I had called.
He asked, "Is she a doctor?" → He asked if she was a doctor.
She asked, "Do they live nearby?" → She asked whether they lived nearby.
words.m: [의문문 간접화법] if 대신 whether 사용 가능

words.w: He asked, "Are you tired?" → He asked if I was tired.
She asked, "Can he help?" → She asked if he could help.
words.m: [의문문 간접화법] 그녀는 그가 도울 수 있는지 물었다.

words.w: He asked, "Have you met him?" → He asked if I had met him.
words.m: [의문문 간접화법] 그는 내가 그를 만난 적 있는지 물었다.

words.w: She asked, "Did you finish?" → She asked if I had finished.
He asked, "Are they coming?" → He asked if they were coming.
She asked, "Does he know?" → She asked if he knew.
He asked, "Will she agree?" → He asked if she would agree.
She asked, "Can you help me?" → She asked if I could help her.
He asked, "Do you understand?" → He asked if I understood.
She asked, "Have they arrived?" → She asked whether they had arrived.
He asked, "Are you happy?" → He asked if I was happy.
She asked, "Did it rain?" → She asked whether it had rained.
He asked, "What is your name?" → He asked what my name was.
She asked, "Where do you live?" → She asked where I lived.
words.m: [의문문 간접화법] 그녀는 내가 어디 사는지 물었다.

words.w: He asked, "When will you come?" → He asked when I would come.
words.m: [의문문 간접화법] 그는 내가 언제 올지 물었다.

words.w: She asked, "Why are you late?" → She asked why I was late.
words.m: [의문문 간접화법] 그녀는 내가 왜 늦었는지 물었다.

words.w: He asked, "How do you feel?" → He asked how I felt.
words.m: [의문문 간접화법] 그는 내 기분이 어떤지 물었다.

words.w: She asked, "Who called?" → She asked who had called.
He asked, "What are you doing?" → He asked what I was doing.
words.m: [의문문 간접화법] 그는 내가 뭘 하는지 물었다.

words.w: She asked, "Where did you go?" → She asked where I had gone.
words.m: [의문문 간접화법] 그녀는 내가 어디 갔는지 물었다.

words.w: He asked, "When did it happen?" → He asked when it had happened.
words.m: [의문문 간접화법] 그는 그 일이 언제 일어났는지 물었다.

words.w: She asked, "Why did he leave?" → She asked why he had left.
words.m: [의문문 간접화법] 그녀는 그가 왜 떠났는지 물었다.

words.w: He asked, "How much is it?" → He asked how much it was.
She asked, "Who is he?" → She asked who he was.
He asked, "What time is it?" → He asked what time it was.
She asked, "How long will you stay?" → She asked how long I would stay.
words.m: [의문문 간접화법] 그녀는 내가 얼마나 머물지 물었다.

words.w: He asked, "Which one do you want?" → He asked which one I wanted.
words.m: [의문문 간접화법] 그는 내가 어느 것을 원하는지 물었다.

words.w: She asked, "What happened?" → She asked what had happened.
words.m: [의문문 간접화법] 그녀는 무슨 일이 있었는지 물었다.

words.w: He asked, "Whose book is this?" → He asked whose book that was.
words.m: [의문문 간접화법] 그는 그것이 누구의 책인지 물었다.

words.w: She asked, "How many students came?" → She asked how many students had come.
words.m: [의문문 간접화법] 그녀는 몇 명의 학생이 왔는지 물었다.

words.w: He asked, "Why are you crying?" → He asked why I was crying.
words.m: [의문문 간접화법] 그는 내가 왜 우는지 물었다.

words.w: She asked, "Where is the station?" → She asked where the station was.
He said, "Open the door." → He told me to open the door.
words.m: [명령문 간접화법] 명령문: told + 목 + to V

words.w: She said to me, "Be quiet." → She told me to be quiet.
words.m: [명령문 간접화법] 그녀는 내게 조용히 하라고 말했다.

words.w: He said, "Don't run." → He told me not to run.
words.m: [명령문 간접화법] 부정 명령: not to V

words.w: She said, "Please help me." → She asked me to help her.
He said, "Study hard." → He told me to study hard.
words.m: [명령문 간접화법] 그는 내게 열심히 공부하라고 말했다.

words.w: She said, "Don't be late." → She told me not to be late.
words.m: [명령문 간접화법] 그녀는 내게 늦지 말라고 말했다.

words.w: He said, "Close the window." → He told me to close the window.
words.m: [명령문 간접화법] 그는 내게 창문을 닫으라고 말했다.

words.w: She said, "Be careful." → She told me to be careful.
He said, "Sit down." → He told me to sit down.
She said, "Don't worry." → She told me not to worry.
words.m: [명령문 간접화법] 그녀는 내게 걱정하지 말라고 말했다.

words.w: He said, "Wait here." → He told me to wait there.
She said, "Please come back." → She asked me to come back.
He said, "Stop talking." → He told me to stop talking.
words.m: [명령문 간접화법] 그는 내게 말을 멈추라고 말했다.

words.w: She said, "Don't forget." → She told me not to forget.
words.m: [명령문 간접화법] 그녀는 내게 잊지 말라고 말했다.

words.w: He said, "Bring it to me." → He told me to bring it to him.
She said, "Don't touch that." → She told me not to touch that.
words.m: [명령문 간접화법] 그녀는 내게 그것을 만지지 말라고 말했다.

words.w: He said, "Let's go." → He suggested going.
She said, "Call me later." → She asked me to call her later.
words.m: [명령문 간접화법] 그녀는 나중에 전화해 달라고 부탁했다.

words.w: He said, "Tell me the truth." → He told me to tell him the truth.
words.m: [명령문 간접화법] 그는 내게 진실을 말하라고 했다.

words.w: She said, "Take this." → She told me to take it.
words.m: [명령문 간접화법] 그녀는 내게 이것을 가져가라고 했다.

words.w: He said, "Don't give up." → He told me not to give up.
words.m: [명령문 간접화법] 그는 내게 포기하지 말라고 했다.

words.w: She said, "Come with me." → She asked me to come with her.
words.m: [명령문 간접화법] 그녀는 내게 같이 가자고 했다.

words.w: He said, "Try again." → He told me to try again.
words.m: [명령문 간접화법] 그는 내게 다시 시도하라고 했다.

words.w: She said, "Don't say anything." → She told me not to say anything.
words.m: [명령문 간접화법] 그녀는 내게 아무 말도 하지 말라고 했다.

words.w: He said, "Please be on time." → He asked me to be on time.
words.m: [명령문 간접화법] 그는 내게 정시에 와 달라고 부탁했다.

words.w: He said, "I am tired." → He said he was tired.
She said, "I live here." → She said she lived there.
He said, "I went." → He said he had gone.
She said, "I have seen it." → She said she had seen it.
He said, "I'll come." → He said he would come.
She said, "I can do it." → She said she could do it.
He said, "I may come." → He said he might come.
She said, "I must leave." → She said she had to leave.
He said, "I'm studying." → He said he was studying.
She said, "I was tired." → She said she had been tired.
He said, "I had finished." → He said he had finished.
She said, "I don't know." → She said she didn't know.
He said, "I'm going to stay." → He said he was going to stay.
words.m: [시제 일치] be going to → was going to

words.w: She said, "I should go." → She said she should go.
He said, "The sun rises in the east." → He said the sun rises in the east.
She said, "Water boils at 100 degrees." → She said water boils at 100 degrees.
He said, "Columbus discovered America." → He said Columbus discovered America.
She said, "I was born in 2000." → She said she was born in 2000.
He said, "I have known her since 2010." → He said he had known her since 2010.
She said, "I'll be there." → She said she would be there.
He said, "I'll come today." → He said he would come that day.
She said, "I'm here now." → She said she was there then.
He said, "I saw her yesterday." → He said he had seen her the day before.
She said, "I'll come tomorrow." → She said she would come the next day.
He said, "This is mine." → He said that was his.
She said, "These are my books." → She said those were her books.
He said, "I did it last week." → He said he had done it the week before.
words.m: [인칭·부사 변화] last week → the week before

words.w: She said, "I'll see you next week." → She said she would see me the following week.
words.m: [인칭·부사 변화] next week → the following week

words.w: He said, "I'm going now." → He said he was going then.
She said, "I met him here." → She said she had met him there.
He said, "I am happy today." → He said he was happy that day.
She said, "We ate this morning." → She said they had eaten that morning.
words.m: [인칭·부사 변화] this morning → that morning, we → they

words.w: He said, "I arrived three days ago." → He said he had arrived three days before.
She said, "I'll call you later." → She said she would call me later.
words.m: [인칭·부사 변화] later 는 그대로 유지 가능

words.w: He said, "I'll be back soon." → He said he would be back soon.
He said, "What a beautiful day!" → He exclaimed how beautiful the day was.
She said, "How kind she is!" → She exclaimed how kind she was.
He said, "Let's go now." → He suggested going then.
She said, "May you be happy." → She wished me to be happy.
words.m: [특수 화법] May + 기원 → wished + to V

words.w: He said, "Thank you." → He thanked me.
She said, "Good morning!" → She greeted me.
He said, "Good-bye." → He said goodbye.
She said, "Let's have dinner." → She suggested having dinner.
He said, "Hurray! We won!" → He shouted that they had won.
She said, "What a shame!" → She exclaimed what a shame it was.
